front: Name the five main model families by what they produce
back: LLMs (text), vision models (understand images), speech models (transcribe/speak), image generators, video generators.
One model handling several formats - e.g. reads a photo and answers in text. Like a colleague who can read, look, and listen.
OpenAI (GPT), Anthropic (Claude), Google DeepMind (Gemini), Meta (Llama), Mistral AI, xAI (Grok).
Open weights: parameters published, anyone can download and run (Llama). Closed: reachable only via the provider's API (GPT, Claude).
Capability vs speed vs cost. Bigger = smarter, slower, pricier. Match the model size to the task.
A standardized test for comparing models. Useful for shortlisting, but gameable - always try models on your own task.

front: The full AI stack in one line
back: Model -> prompts & context -> tools -> RAG -> memory -> APIs -> workflows & agents -> MCP connecting it all.
